vision: A lightweight web ATS for small recruiting agencies that replaces the spreadsheet-and-email pipeline with one shared board, and auto-ranks candidates the moment resumes are dumped into a job by delegating to TalentOS.
problem: Small recruiting agencies (2-10 recruiters) run their whole hiring pipeline in spreadsheets and email: resumes get lost in inboxes, two recruiters unknowingly work the same candidate, and no one can show a client where their candidates stand without digging.

target_users.label: Agency recruiter
target_users.description: Runs multiple client jobs at once; lives in the pipeline daily.

target_users.label: Agency client (hiring company)
target_users.description: Wants a read-only view of where their candidates stand.

goals.text: Replace spreadsheet/email chaos with one shared pipeline every recruiter can see.
Auto-rank candidates on resume dump by delegating to TalentOS, so recruiters get an evidence-backed shortlist without manual triage.
Eliminate double-contact — no two recruiters unknowingly work the same candidate.
Give clients a live read-only view of their candidates' status.
Provide a fairness/defensibility surface for the automated rank. [added by amendment 001, accepts security challenge on g2]

non_goals.text: Building our own resume-ranking engine — TalentOS already does this (see c4).
Client read-only view (g4) is NOT in the MVP — it is v1.1, first fast-follow. [added by amendment 001, es1=B: recruiter-only MVP fits c2]

constraints.text: Solo builder plus a friend who does frontend.

TalentOS available as a local-first ranking service: project-scoped REST API (create project, PUT job [LLM-parses], POST resumes, POST /process, POST /rank -> RankingResult with evidence, strengths/gaps explanations, interview-kit gen). Coupling tightness left to Engineer.
TalentOS runs locally against Ollama + FAISS (local-first, ~$0 infra).

budget.text: Unspecified; local-first implies ~$0 infra for MVP.

success_metrics.metric: Time-to-shortlist: recruiter goes from resume dump to a ranked, evidence-backed shortlist
Pipeline adoption: at least one agency runs live jobs end-to-end in the tool instead of spreadsheets
Zero double-contact incidents once the collision guard is live

assumptions.text: TalentOS's REST API is stable enough to build against as-is, without forking it.
Recruiters will trust TalentOS's ranking enough to act on it — conditional on seeing the evidence.
Agency clients actually want a self-serve read-only portal (vs. an emailed status).

risks.text: MVP value (g2) is coupled to TalentOS; if its API shifts or it's unavailable, ranking breaks.
Client view + TalentOS integration is a lot to land in 'a few weeks'.

gaps_to_clarify.text: Auth model for client access — magic link, scoped tokens, separate portal? Needed for the client-view build.
How resumes enter the system — manual upload only, or email ingest?
